full_text: Hey Explorer launch day up for it let's let's just do another gear check we're at a critical juncture so let's go over what's in store we have a full roster of equipment to wire up number one our dip switch to toggle all system functions and initiate the countdown of our seven segment display number two our OED which will serve as our control panel display and number three our trusty buzzer to keep us Alert in case of any safety concerns our task is immense our hearts are stout and our minds sharper than ever so let's roll up our sleeves strap in and prepare for liftoff the vastness of space beckons and we will answer it call to paraphrase an ancient Teran to close to Infinity but never Beyond we're going to put together our OLED seven segment display dip switch and passive buzzer into one masterful circuit this intricate Symphony of Hardware will be the central nervous system for our liftoff the OLED will serve as our main control panel display the seven segment display will take care of our crucial Countdown the dip switch will be our trigger to initiate all system functions and the passive buzzer will be our trusty Watchdog alerting us of any anomalies today we are adding the grand finale to our code but before we initiate the spectacular conflagration that will power our Escape we need to start with the smaller Sparks it all begins with our dip switch dip switch three systems all need to be on before we're go to launch timer is wired up correctly and well to the seven segments eyes on the clock and then we'll see what happens there's some instructional material as usual check things over I'll do a flight check you know got to make sure good luck hello everybody and welcome back to day 24 of the 30 days lawon space code explanations today we're going to be taking what we did on day 23 adding in a wonderful little dip switch and a passive buzzer onto our project before your lift off later today now before we get into the full bit of the code I want to talk to you guys about how the program will work from a higher level perspective when you first start the program all of the dip switch positions should be set to the off position so that very first state that we can possibly be in out of five is going to be called initialization if I go down here we'll see this wonderful little new struct that we've cre created that you guys can actually look at here and this is the very first state initialization when all the dip switches are off now pending is where we're waiting for all of them to be turned on once they are all on we have the following we have countdown now if at any point during this countdown we turn off one of the dip switches we're going to go to the abort State because we just accidentally cut off the state here and finally if we do actually make the countdown all the way to zero we'll finally enter this liftoff State here but if at again if at any point we decide to turn that lever off into the off position we will abort the countdown and we'll have to then restart it now let's go over the code from the top to the bottom and let's take out the stuff that we talked about in the days before so that we don't have to restate that this obviously we've talked about this in every piece of code this is included by default by the Arduino IDE but we're just excluding putting it in here explicitly just so you know that it's there here's a seven segment display the graphics libraries and of course we talked about the Define macros yesterday and how they convert things to minutes and seconds yesterday down here below we have the pins that are required for the seven segment display as well as the Constructor for the seven segment display so we can make that and actually call the functions from the library down here below we now have the pins for the dip switch is this is new for today this just simply defines what pins we're going to be using pin n will be for our lever uh thrust pin this one's going to be for our system lever and this one's going to be for our confirmation lever down here below we have the buzzer pin is going to be equal to six and we're putting that on a pulse withth modulation pin so that we can obviously play all the various tones using pulse withth modulation on pin six talked about this yesterday don't need to go over this again this is just simply making similar to what we did with the seven segment display same exact idea here for our Lander OLED we are also going to be defining how big our Lander graphic is so in the very bottom right of our program we will have a Lander that when we finally do take off we'll have a little animation that shoots itself up to the top of the screen now we are defining how big that is in bits and we have 25 by 20 down here say segment display defining the done value again talked about this yesterday and on day 16 here we have the countdown in milliseconds now one thing that we changed on this line of code here from yesterday as you probably have remembered yesterday we had the countdown it was 5 Seconds time 1,000 but this time we're changing it to 70 so it would be a minute and 10 seconds uh that's what it should display but however we put this UL here and what this is going to do is this is going to going to make it so that we have it be done as an unsigned long remember when we're talking about data types signed variables can go negative unsigned variables can't go negative um there's no sign bit at the end which allows it to determine if it's a positive or negative number so unassigned variables don't have that they don't have that sign and they always by default just use positive numbers or obviously zero can be done as well so if we're using unsigned numbers we're just making sure because it's a countdown there's no reason to go to negative 1G -2 neg3 you know obviously as for a countdown reason so we're using unsigned and we're putting this here just to make sure that there's no way that this can accidentally be misconstrued uh when we want to be using onside numbers down here below we have the en that shows the five different states that we can possibly be in throughout this program now depending on what state we're in we're obviously going to be executing things differently so an enumerator is a Comm and to Define multiple related values where distinguishing between the different values is important but the actual values are not so this is perfect for something like this when we're trying to talk about the various states that our program can be in each name is then given its own value and in this case it starts with zero so this would be zero 1 2 3 and four so out of the five states it's you can think of it is almost like an array of uh variables 0 1 2 3 and four so now we've talked about the enes let's hop down to void setup and let's go on to here to talk about what's new for today now obviously we're talking about serial monitor set to seven segment display settings set the OLED settings all stuff we talked about yesterday finally we're setting all the dip switch pins as inputs and we're just going to clear the old display that's it for the setup let's jump down to Loop one thing we'll notice though is that this time we're going through Loop we are going to have a slight issue with how we're doing things the reason we'll have a slight issue is because in order to keep the beeping and animation steady we really have to make sure that each time we go through the loop is the same amount of time meaning we can't have one time going through the loop be quicker than the other times otherwise it could break things and the way that the functions in our Graphics library and tone libraries work they don't really care they will break if you're not careful about it so the way we're going to be doing this is we're basically going to make sure that the minimum time that we have to go through Loop is going to be about 200 milliseconds and we're going to set a defined value basically limiting how long it takes to go through each time occasionally Loops may take longer but this constant defines the minimum time each Loop will take at minimum to run so each iteration going through this whole bit has to take at least at at the minimum 200 milliseconds so that that way we can make sure that anytime that we call any of our tone or Graphics things it has enough time to execute everything it needs to execute especially when we're using the first p uh first page next page stuff saving all that stuff for memory saving reasons we need to make sure that we actually have enough time to then execute that since it does take more time to execute to save the memory we need for the program down here in Loop we're going to obviously initialize our static variables all the way at the top and we're going to obviously start here in the initialization State remember that en we had before initialization state is where we're going to start then we're going to do the following as you remember last time we had the following Loop start time these are obviously just variables to keep track of where we are in the loop and of course we even have this Loop toggle equals true which is just going to be defined to say whether we are going to be tone or blink a text this way through the loop so every time that we go through this loop we're going to change this variable here to onof on off so we can keep the looking flashy and Blinky up at the top with our text and beeping tones and so on here we have all the levers um where we're going to read the state of each lever before we decide what we're going to do next now we're after we do all the reading of what we have we're simply going to update the display with the current status of our liftoff sequence this is either going to be on or off this is either going to be on or off and this is either going to be on or off and then we have obviously one of the five liftoff states that we can be in as well so we're going to quickly update the Lander display this calls a new function we created this function down below if we go past all this stuff here we can find the update OLED display on this display we set the max Lander speed that it can go up to the top this is our little funny little rocket ship we have in the bottom right corner corner of our display that will be going up upon execution we're simply setting the Speed Max Speed to be five we don't want to go too fast otherwise it'll just appear off disappear off the screen way too quickly and you won't even be able to see the animation down below we have void update Lander display taking in those things and then obviously depending on what they are we just want to set the up the display to say what it is properly expiration Lander left off sequence at the top and finally we simply just say if it is on we want to say that it is in state liftoff aborted if it is in liftoff then we want to say liftoff but if we accidentally abort it then it's aborted else we want to be able to display as well the is if it's on we want to put it on otherwise if it's off we want to put it off in programming this is going to seem like a very very awkward thing to look at this here is is a very interesting unique way to basically write if thrusters level is true everything after so you can think of it this way the question mark is essentially like the brackets right so if this is true then you're going to do the very first one else you're going to do whatever is after this colon here this is a very shorthanded way of writing that entire if statement but using strings this is basically going to determine what we're going to display so if it's on we want to put up we want to say it's on otherwise if letter is off we want to say it's off if this is on we want to say on otherwise off on on off off same exact thing for all three of them and we obviously have the all of those being displayed on our olded panel and then we just simply update the display with that information finally we also have this helper function that basically turns whatever state it is into a string so if we're in the state of an it we want to return the string in it pending pending countdown we want to make it active liftoff we can change this to complete in abort we're just going to keep abort obviously we use this up here so lift off to string and we can get the state of that it's in and we just return that as a string and push that onto our display this is the functionality we discussed yesterday to display the counter on our seven segment display this draws a string on our OLED and this obviously displays our Lander draws all the boxes and everything to displayed in the bottom right corner on our display if we go back to the loop go back to where we left from here under the update Lander display after we've updated the Lander display to display all the information that we need to have it display we're basically going to go down and we're going to make some decision making decision making based off of what state we're in the first state we could possibly be in is is initialization so if we're initializing we want to check to see if all three switches are off false and if that's the case then we can turn off the beeping next we have to check to see if we're in the pending State meaning that at least one of them is turned on and if that's the case then if let's just say all three of them are now turned on at this execution throughout the loop then we want to go down here and we want to make sure that we change eventually this state to countdown finally we're going to go and we're going to say else if liftoff state is equal to countdown meaning that all three are now in the on position we'll simply be keeping track of the state of the countdown timer and also in the off chance that one of them happens to be turned off if not thrust lever or not system lever or not confirm lever if one of them happens to be taken off then we want to switch to the abort State immediately because obviously we did not complete the countdown and if we did successfully get all the way down and all the way to zero on our liftoff sequence we want to set the segment on our seven segment display to done play it to do tone and obviously update the Lander and make our little animation thing in the bottom right go up to the top make the little ship animation go up and with that that should be everything that's required for you to take your ship up to space today Cadet for day 24 of the lost and space code explanations I look forward to seeing you guys in orbit see you then welcome to the day 24 wiring example video all our wiring programming and simulation practice has finally brought us here to launch day in this video I'll walk you through the hardware setup and operations that should closely resemble your experience as you prepare for today's liftoff this should should provide you with a final chance to check your systems and prepare yourself for the events of the launch sequence including any contingencies that might crop up that would require an abort or recycle to start off let's look over the complete wiring setup as we had on the previous day we are including the seven segment countdown display and the O LED Graphics screen in our system in addition we're adding the buzzer module to provide us with audible feedback and warning indications during the launch sequence much of the wiring today resembles the previous days but we've rearranged the power and ground distribution just a little bit in order to service all of our devices in a clean way as before we've spread things out a little bit onto a secondary breadboard in order to make things easier to see here in the video but you may well have your circuit wired more compactly than this in any case you'll notice that in this example we've actually provided two ground buses it's really just one ground bus connected together as one but we're using both the lower ground bus here on the bottom side of the breadboard and the upper ground bus as well you'll see that the lower ground bus right here is connected by a black jumper wire to the hero ground and this lower ground bus provides the ground side connection for our three switch logic resistors and so this is uh a convenient place for us to access ground here we uh also have the uh ground connections on the upper ground bus you can just see it up here on the top side which is used to provide the negative or ground connection right here for our buzzer and we have a jumper that connects over to the left side to provide ground for our two display modules we're able in this example to get away with just a single plus 5vt bus and this is distributed from the hero 5vt Supply through this R line and connects to the upper bus line the red bus line here along the top side now you'll see that it supplies our three switch circuits right here through these three red lines so these power are switch circuits which we'll use to detect status on those lines and in addition we have a jumper that extends the 5 volts here over to the left to provide 5vt Power once again for our two displays on the left side so now the last things in terms of wiring that we need to check are our output pin connections the positive signal for the warning buzzer connects to Hero pin 6 through this yellow wire right here that connects the positive terminal of the buzzer to Hero pin six our three switch status lines switch one switch two and switch three are in turn connected to the hero pins 9 8 and 7 and those in this example are connected by these three wires this orange brown and green wire connect to those three hero pins and finally as we had before the seven segment clock and Dio lines are connected to pins five and four down here on the hero through these two lines through the uh brown and white lines that connect to those pins and the OLED this display right here the OLED clock and data lines are fed by the heroes analog A5 and A4 pins that's through these this green and blue wire so that pretty much summarizes the hardware setup and the wiring necessary for this uh launch sequence so now let's run through the launch countdown sequence we'll load the sketch and uh we'll run through a simulated launch of course it won't be simulated for you so that's why we want to be careful to uh figure everything out here in advance now in that Spirit I'm actually going to start off right now with an intentional mistake what I'm going to do is I'm going to leave one of the control switches in the armed position rather than in its correct off position which we would expect for initialization so let's go ahead and load the sketch and see what happens in this circumstance so going to the IDE go ahead and upload the sketch uploading and compiling is done and we have a warning buzzer and we have an indication that we are not able to begin the liftoff sequence we have a problem so we notice oh yes we did not have the switches properly configured so I'm going to go ahead and switch those back into their proper off configuration so that we can begin the sequence properly I guess what I'll do right now is I'll reset the hero so we can start from a clean start but now with the switches in the correct position and there we go we now have a liftoff sequence prompt that says the countdown is pending and so all we need to do now is to in order arm the three switches so first we'll do the Thruster switch switch one we get an indication on the OLED that that system is on second switch for systems is on and confirmed and so finally to confirm launch will uh throw the final switch into the on position and we should see the initiation of our countdown so here we go switch is on and countdown clock is underway so at this point we can go ahead and uh wait for the countdown to run down and uh proceed with launch however let's anticipate a possible problem let's suppose that prior to launch commit we notice that there are something not quite right perhaps there's something loose in the cabin or or or something uh isn't really ready for launch so we can go ahead and abort the launch by flipping one of the switches back to off and sure enough we've aborted the launch the countdown has halted and we uh are going to await further instructions so I'll go ahead turn all the switches back to off and we'll reset the hero one more time and this time we'll run it through to a nominal countdown and launch and hopefully that'll cover all the possibilities that we're likely to see when we actually go through with a launch so resetting the hero we're back in the nominal countdown pending mode the switches are ready to go so let we fixed all the problems that we had so let's go ahead with our countdown and launch first switch is on second switch is on and now for final confirmation countdown has begun so now all we have to do is wait for the automattic IC System to bring us through the final count and to launch and we're in the final seconds of the countdown 9 8 7 6 5 4 3 2 1 lach and we're finally on our way good luck with your launch if all goes well the next time we talk to you you'll be safely in orbit so we'll see you then the time has finally come Explorer on my mark you'll flip the three levers in sequence to initiate the countdown to launch this is what we have been waiting for we'll only have one shot at this flip lever one to initiate Thruster power now well done flip the second lever to power supplemental launch circuits on my mark now you're doing perfectly so far Explorer now ready yourself to flip the third lever and initiate our countdown on my mark now the sequence successfully initiated Explorer performing failsafe Diagnostics data readouts normal diagnostic shows power system are humming beautifully your moments from freedom in the stars hold tight now Explorer ready your body and mind for liftoff go I didn't think we'd make it you did great Explorer I mean you covered so much code and wiring in such a short span of time you rock we're in space where we're meant to be well not quite where we're meant to be but once we're at the Mother Ship we'll oh oh no
